paper_title: PLMGH: What Matters in PLM-GNN Hybrids for Code Classification and Vulnerability Detection

perturbations.why_wrong: The paper defines the architecture as a 'feature injection' pipeline where PLM outputs are fed into GNNs (PLM $\rightarrow$ GNN). A bidirectional arrow implies a cyclic or simultaneous interaction which contradicts the described sequential flow.

The context describes a 'grid' of combinations between PLMs and GNNs to evaluate pairings. Using an addition sign (+) instead of a product/cross sign ($\times$) is notationally inconsistent with the concept of a Cartesian product of models and architectures mentioned later in the paper.

The pipeline describes injecting PLM features into a GNN. Reversing the arrow suggests the GNN is being injected into the PLM, which contradicts the subsequent steps (i) through (iii) where PLM representations are extracted first to serve as inputs for the GNN.
The text refers to a 'PLM-GNN grid' for tuning and comparison. A minus sign is mathematically incorrect for representing a set of combinations or a matrix of experimental configurations.

The text explicitly describes the learning rate as 'small'. A learning rate of 0.1 ($1\mathrm{e}{-1}$) is considered very large for finetuning pretrained language models and contradicts the qualitative description provided in the same sentence.

The section describes 'frozen' PLM representations being used as features for GNNs. A bidirectional arrow ($\leftrightarrow$) implies a mutual exchange or joint training, which contradicts the statement that the PLMs are frozen feature extractors.

The starting index $(i-1)S$ is 0 when $i=1$, but the sequence of tokens $T$ is typically 1-indexed in this notation, and more importantly, the stride logic for overlapping fragments usually starts at index 1 to align with the first token.

The text explicitly states we identify tokens whose spans 'overlap' the interval to obtain a node semantic vector; changing this to 'outside' contradicts the textual description of the alignment process.
The text explicitly describes the operation as 'average their representations'. An average is the sum divided by the count $|I_v|$, not the square of the count $|I_v|^2$.

The standard definition of a normalized Laplacian matrix, which the text identifies as $L_{\mathrm{sym}}$, requires subtracting the normalized adjacency matrix from the identity matrix, not adding it.
The text states that each node $v$ has a node-type embedding $q_v$. Mapping $q_{v+1}$ to node $v$ creates a misalignment where a node uses the type information of a different node.

The text states that the threshold $\tau^\star$ is calibrated on the 'validation set' and then applied to the 'test sets'. Changing the subscript to 'test' creates a data leakage error where the threshold is optimized on the same data used for reporting final metrics.
